description_long: This is a cylindrical vase made from a light-colored, stone-like material, likely alabaster or marble, with a thick, rounded rim and a hollow interior. The surface displays a natural, variegated pattern with vertical streaks and patches of beige, tan, and off-white, giving it an aged, weathered appearance. The rim is slightly flared and smooth, while the body is subtly faceted and tapers gently towards the base. Some sides show more pronounced discoloration and mineral deposits, suggesting exposure to the elements or long-term use. The vase has a substantial, solid look, with no visible handles or decorative reliefs, emphasizing its utilitarian yet elegant form. Its overall finish is matte and rough, with visible textural irregularities and mineral veining.
description: A cylindrical, stone-like vase with a thick, rounded rim and natural beige and white streaks, featuring a weathered, matte finish. The surface is rough and variegated, with no handles or ornate details.
description_view_0: This view highlights a large, oval patch of brownish discoloration on the body, bordered by lighter stone, and shows the thick, rounded rim.
description_view_1: This side displays vertical streaks of beige and white, with a more uniform coloration and less pronounced patching.
description_view_2: This view reveals a patchy, spotted brown area on the body, with several white spots and a slightly more angular profile.
description_view_3: This side is similar to view 1, with vertical beige and white streaks, but appears slightly lighter and more uniform.
